text: Many workplaces now use artificial intelligence to handle repetitive tasks, a process called automation. For example, AI can sort emails, manage inventory, or process data much faster than humans. This allows employees to focus on more creative and complex work. Productivity tools powered by AI also help workers plan their schedules, generate reports, and even improve customer service. However, this does not mean machines replace humans completely. Instead, human-AI collaboration is becoming the new standard. People guide the AI’s decisions and solve problems it cannot handle. As a result, some jobs change or disappear, but new roles emerge that require skills in managing and interpreting AI. The key is to adapt and learn.
